version.description:
As a safety measure, the new version number of the layout must
be specified here

layout.description: Details about the new cluster layout

message.description:
Plain-text information about the layout computation
(do not try to parse this)

allOf.description: Structured statistics about the layout computation

allowMissingData.description:
Allow the skip even if a quorum of nodes could not be found for
the data among the remaining nodes

version.description:
Version number of the layout to assume is currently up-to-date.
This will generally be the current layout version.

ackUpdated.description: Nodes for which the ACK update tracker has been updated to `version`

syncUpdated.description:
If `allow_missing_data` is set,
nodes for which the SYNC update tracker has been updated to `version`

gatewayNodes.description: Number of nodes with a gateway role in this layout version

storageNodes.description: Number of nodes with an assigned storage capacity in this layout version

version.description: Version number of this layout version

effectiveCapacity.description: The final effective capacity of the cluster, accounting for replication

effectiveZoneRedundancy.description: The zone redundancy factor achieved by this layout

lowPartitionSize.description: Warning flag indicating when partitions are very small

lowUsableCapacity.description:
Warning flag indicating that the raw node capacity could not be used
effectively

partitionSize.description: The size of a partition, in bytes

previousPartitionSize.description: The size of a partition, in bytes, in the previous layout

totalCapacity.description: The total raw capacity of nodes

totalMovedPartitions.description: The total number of partitions that will be moved to a new storage node

usableCapacity.description: The portion of total raw node capacity that is used by partitions

newPartitions.description: The number of partitions that are newly replicated on this node

storedPartitions.description: The number of partitions that are replicated on this node

tags.description: The node's tags as defined in the layout

usableCapacity.description: The portion of the node's raw capacity that is used by partitions it stores

nodes.description: Per-node storage statistics for nodes in this zone

totalCapacity.description: The total raw capacity of nodes in this zone

totalReplicatedPartitions.description: The total number of partition replicas in this zone

uniquePartitions.description: The number of unique partitions that have at least one replica in this zone

usableCapacity.description: The used portion of the raw capacity of nodes in this zones

error.description: An error message if Garage did not manage to connect to this node

success.description: `true` if Garage managed to connect to this node

secretToken.description:
The secret bearer token. **CAUTION:** This token will be shown only
ONCE, so this value MUST be remembered somewhere, or the token
will be unusable.

expiration.description: Expiration time and date, formatted according to RFC 3339

expired.description: Whether this admin token is expired already

id.description: Identifier of the admin token (which is also a prefix of the full bearer token)

name.description: Name of the admin API token

scope.description:
Scope of the admin API token, a list of admin endpoint names (such as
`GetClusterStatus`, etc), or the special value `*` to allow all
admin endpoints

bytes.description: Total number of bytes used by objects in this bucket

globalAliases.description: List of global aliases for this bucket

keys.description: List of access keys that have permissions granted on this bucket

lifecycleRules.description: Object lifecycle rules for this bucket

objects.description: Number of objects in this bucket

quotas.description: Quotas that apply to this bucket

unfinishedMultipartUploadBytes.description: Total number of bytes used by unfinished multipart uploads in this bucket

unfinishedMultipartUploadParts.description: Number of parts in unfinished multipart uploads in this bucket

unfinishedMultipartUploads.description: Number of unfinished multipart uploads in this bucket

unfinishedUploads.description: Number of unfinished uploads in this bucket

websiteAccess.description: Whether website access is enabled for this bucket

connectedNodes.description: the number of nodes this Garage node currently has an open connection to

knownNodes.description: the number of nodes this Garage node has had a TCP connection to since the daemon started

partitions.description: the total number of partitions of the data (currently always 256)

partitionsAllOk.description: the number of partitions for which we are connected to all storage nodes responsible of storing it

partitionsQuorum.description: the number of partitions for which a quorum of write nodes is available

status.description:
One of `healthy`, `degraded` or `unavailable`:
- `healthy`: Garage node is connected to all storage nodes
- `degraded`: Garage node is not connected to all storage nodes, but a quorum of write nodes is available for all partitions
- `unavailable`: a quorum of write nodes is not available for some partitions

storageNodes.description: the number of storage nodes currently registered in the cluster layout

storageNodesUp.description: the number of storage nodes to which a connection is currently open

currentVersion.description: The current version number of the cluster layout

minAck.description:
All nodes in the cluster are aware of layout versions up to
this version number (at least)

parameters.description: Layout parameters used when the current layout was computed

partitionSize.description: The size, in bytes, of one Garage partition (= a shard)

roles.description: List of nodes that currently have a role in the cluster layout

allOf.description:
Layout parameters to use when computing the next version of
the cluster layout

stagedRoleChanges.description:
List of nodes that will have a new role or whose role will be
removed in the next version of the cluster layout

version.description: The current version number of the cluster layout

bucketCount.description: number of buckets in the cluster

dataAvail.description: available storage space for object data in the entire cluster, in bytes

freeform.description:
cluster statistics as a free-form string, kept for compatibility with nodes
running older v2.x versions of garage

incompleteAvailInfo.description:
true if the available storage space statistics are imprecise due to missing
information of disconnected nodes. When this is the case, the actual
space available in the cluster might be lower than the reported values.

metadataAvail.description: available storage space for object metadata in the entire cluster, in bytes

totalObjectBytes.description:
total size of objects stored in all buckets, before compression, deduplication and
replication (this is NOT equivalent to actual disk usage in the cluster)

totalObjectCount.description: total number of objects stored in all buckets

layoutVersion.description: Current version number of the cluster layout

nodes.description:
List of nodes that are either currently connected, part of the
current cluster layout, or part of an older cluster layout that
is still active in the cluster (being drained).

hash.description: Hash (blake2 sum) of the block's data

offset.description: Offset of this block within the part

partNumber.description: Part number of the part containing this block, for multipart uploads

bucketId.description: ID of the bucket containing the inspected object

versions.description: List of versions currently stored for this object

aborted.description: Whether this is an aborted upload

blocks.description: List of data blocks for this object version

deleteMarker.description:
Whether this version is a delete marker (a tombstone indicating that a previous version of
the object has been deleted)

encrypted.description: Whether this object version was created with SSE-C encryption

headers.description: Metadata (HTTP headers) associated with this object version

inline.description: Whether the object's data is stored inline (for small objects)

size.description: Size of the object, in bytes

timestamp.description: Creation timestamp of this object version

uploading.description: Whether this object version is still uploading

capacity.description:
Capacity (in bytes) assigned by the cluster administrator,
absent for gateway nodes

storedPartitions.description:
Number of partitions stored on this node
(a result of the layout computation)

tags.description: List of tags assigned by the cluster administrator

usableCapacity.description:
Capacity (in bytes) that is actually usable on this node in the current
layout, which is equal to `stored_partitions` × `partition_size`

zone.description: Zone name assigned by the cluster administrator

zoneRedundancy.description: Minimum number of zones in which a data partition must be replicated

garageFeatures.description: build-time features enabled for this garage release

garageVersion.description: garage version running on this node

rustVersion.description: rustc version with which this garage release was compiled

node statistics as a free-form string, kept for compatibility with nodes
running older v2.x versions of garage

Scope of the admin API token, a list of admin endpoint names (such as
`GetClusterStatus`, etc), or the special value `*` to allow all
admin endpoints. **WARNING:** Granting a scope of `CreateAdminToken` or
`UpdateAdminToken` trivially allows for privilege escalation, and is thus
functionally equivalent to granting a scope of `*`.

Permissions to deny for the key

neverExpires.description: Set the access key to never expire

oneOf.description:
Partitions must be replicated in at least this number of
distinct zones.

atLeast.description:
Partitions must be replicated in at least this number of
distinct zones.

oneOf.description:
Partitions must be replicated in as many zones as possible:
as many zones as there are replicas, if there are enough distinct
zones, or at least one in each zone otherwise.

info.description:
Administrate your Garage cluster programmatically, including status, layout, keys, buckets, and maintenance tasks.

*Disclaimer: This API may change in future Garage versions. Read the changelog and upgrade your scripts before upgrading. Additionally, this specification is early stage and can contain bugs, so be careful and please report any issues on our issue tracker.*
info.title: Garage administration API

get.description:
Static website domain name check. Checks whether a bucket is configured to serve
a static website for the requested domain. This is used by reverse proxies such
as Caddy or Tricot, to avoid requesting TLS certificates for domain names that
do not correspond to an actual website.

parameters.description: The domain name to check for
parameters.name: domain

200.description: The domain name redirects to a static website bucket

400.description: No static website bucket exists for this domain

get.description:
Check cluster health. The status code returned by this function indicates
whether this Garage daemon can answer API requests.
Garage will return `200 OK` even if some storage nodes are disconnected,
as long as it is able to have a quorum of nodes for read and write operations.

200.description: Garage is able to answer requests

503.description: This Garage daemon is not able to handle requests

200.description: Garage daemon metrics exported in Prometheus format

post.description: Add an alias for the target bucket.  This can be either a global or a local alias, depending on which fields are specified.

200.description: Returns exhaustive information about the bucket

post.description:
⚠️ **DISCLAIMER**: Garage's developers are aware that this endpoint has an unconventional semantic. Be extra careful when implementing it, its behavior is not obvious.

Allows a key to do read/write/owner operations on a bucket.

Flags in permissions which have the value true will be activated. Other flags will remain unchanged (ie. they will keep their internal value).

For example, if you set read to true, the key will be allowed to read the bucket.
If you set it to false, the key will keeps its previous read permission.
If you want to disallow read for the key, check the DenyBucketKey operation.

200.description: Returns exhaustive information about the bucket

post.description:
Applies to the cluster the layout changes currently registered as staged layout changes.

*Note: do not try to parse the `message` field of the response, it is given as an array of string specifically because its format is not stable.*

200.description: The updated cluster layout has been applied in the cluster

post.description: Removes all incomplete multipart uploads that are older than the specified number of seconds.

200.description: The bucket was cleaned up successfully

post.description: Force progress in layout update trackers

200.description: Request has been taken into account

post.description: Instructs this Garage node to connect to other Garage nodes at specified `<node_id>@<net_address>`. `node_id` is generated automatically on node start.

200.description: The request has been handled correctly but it does not mean that all connection requests succeeded; some might have fail, you need to check the body!

post.description: Creates a new admin API token

Creates a new bucket, either with a global alias, a local one, or no alias at all.
Technically, you can also specify both `globalAlias` and `localAlias` and that would create two aliases.

200.description: Returns exhaustive information about the bucket

post.description: Creates a new API access key.
Instruct one or several nodes to take a snapshot of their metadata databases.

parameters.description: Node ID to query, or `*` for all nodes, or `self` for the node responding to the request
parameters.name: node

post.description: Delete an admin API token from the cluster, revoking all its permissions.

parameters.description: Admin API token ID
parameters.name: id

post.description:
Deletes a storage bucket. A bucket cannot be deleted if it is not empty.

**Warning:** this will delete all aliases associated with the bucket!

parameters.description: ID of the bucket to delete
parameters.name: id

post.description: Delete a key from the cluster. Its access will be removed from all the buckets. Buckets are not automatically deleted and can be dangling. You should manually delete them before.

parameters.description: Access key ID
parameters.name: id

post.description:
⚠️ **DISCLAIMER**: Garage's developers are aware that this endpoint has an unconventional semantic. Be extra careful when implementing it, its behavior is not obvious.

Denies a key from doing read/write/owner operations on a bucket.

Flags in permissions which have the value true will be deactivated. Other flags will remain unchanged.

For example, if you set read to true, the key will be denied from reading.
If you set read to false,  the key will keep its previous permissions.
If you want the key to have the reading permission, check the AllowBucketKey operation.

200.description: Returns exhaustive information about the bucket

get.description:
Return information about a specific admin API token.
You can search by specifying the exact token identifier (`id`) or by specifying a pattern (`search`).

parameters.description: Admin API token ID
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Partial token ID or name to search for
parameters.name: search

post.description: Get detailed information about a data block stored on a Garage node, including all object versions and in-progress multipart uploads that contain a reference to this block.

parameters.description: Node ID to query, or `*` for all nodes, or `self` for the node responding to the request
parameters.name: node

get.description:
Given a bucket identifier (`id`) or a global alias (`alias`), get its information.
It includes its aliases, its web configuration, keys that have some permissions
on it, some statistics (number of objects, size), number of dangling multipart uploads,
and its quotas (if any).

parameters.description: Exact bucket ID to look up
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Global alias of bucket to look up
parameters.name: globalAlias

parameters.description: Partial ID or alias to search for
parameters.name: search

200.description: Returns exhaustive information about the bucket

get.description: Returns the global status of the cluster, the number of connected nodes (over the number of known ones), the number of healthy storage nodes (over the declared ones), and the number of healthy partitions (over the total).

Returns the cluster's current layout, including:

- Currently configured cluster layout
- Staged changes to the cluster layout

*Capacity is given in bytes*
Returns the history of layouts in the cluster

Fetch global cluster statistics.

*Note: do not try to parse the `freeform` field of the response, it is given as a string specifically because its format is not stable.*

Returns the cluster's current status, including:

- ID of the node being queried and its version of the Garage daemon
- Live nodes
- Currently configured cluster layout
- Staged changes to the cluster layout

*Capacity is given in bytes*
Return information about the calling admin API token.

Return information about a specific key like its identifiers, its permissions and buckets on which it has permissions.
You can search by specifying the exact key identifier (`id`) or by specifying a pattern (`search`).

For confidentiality reasons, the secret key is not returned by default: you must pass the `showSecretKey` query parameter to get it.

parameters.description: Access key ID
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Partial key ID or name to search for
parameters.name: search

parameters.description: Whether to return the secret access key
parameters.name: showSecretKey

get.description: Return information about the Garage daemon running on one or several nodes.

parameters.description: Node ID to query, or `*` for all nodes, or `self` for the node responding to the request
parameters.name: node

get.description:
Fetch statistics for one or several Garage nodes.

*Note: do not try to parse the `freeform` field of the response, it is given as a string specifically because its format is not stable.*

parameters.description: Node ID to query, or `*` for all nodes, or `self` for the node responding to the request
parameters.name: node

post.description: Get information about the specified background worker on one or several cluster nodes.

parameters.description: Node ID to query, or `*` for all nodes, or `self` for the node responding to the request
parameters.name: node

post.description: Fetch values of one or several worker variables, from one or several cluster nodes.

parameters.description: Node ID to query, or `*` for all nodes, or `self` for the node responding to the request
parameters.name: node

post.description:
Imports an existing API key. This feature must only be used for migrations and backup restore.

**Do not use it to generate custom key identifiers or you will break your Garage cluster.**

get.description:
Returns detailed information about an object in a bucket, including its internal state in Garage.

This API call can be used to list the data blocks referenced by an object,
as well as to view metadata associated to the object.

This call may return a list of more than one version for the object, for instance in the
case where there is a currently stored version of the object, and a newer version whose
upload is in progress and not yet finished.

200.description: Returns exhaustive information about the object

post.description: Launch a repair operation on one or several cluster nodes.

parameters.description: Node ID to query, or `*` for all nodes, or `self` for the node responding to the request
parameters.name: node

get.description: Returns all admin API tokens in the cluster.

200.description: Returns info about all admin API tokens

get.description: List data blocks that are currently in an errored state on one or several Garage nodes.

parameters.description: Node ID to query, or `*` for all nodes, or `self` for the node responding to the request
parameters.name: node

get.description: List all the buckets on the cluster with their UUID and their global and local aliases.

200.description: Returns the UUID of all the buckets and all their aliases

get.description: Returns all API access keys in the cluster.

200.description: Returns the key identifier (aka `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`) and its associated, human friendly, name if any (otherwise return an empty string)

post.description: List background workers currently running on one or several cluster nodes.

parameters.description: Node ID to query, or `*` for all nodes, or `self` for the node responding to the request
parameters.name: node

post.description:
Computes a new layout taking into account the staged parameters, and returns it with detailed statistics. The new layout is not applied in the cluster.

*Note: do not try to parse the `message` field of the response, it is given as an array of string specifically because its format is not stable.*

Purge references to one or several missing data blocks.

This will remove all objects and in-progress multipart uploads that contain the specified data block(s). The objects will be permanently deleted from the buckets in which they appear. Use with caution.

parameters.description: Node ID to query, or `*` for all nodes, or `self` for the node responding to the request
parameters.name: node

post.description: Remove an alias for the target bucket.  This can be either a global or a local alias, depending on which fields are specified.

200.description: Returns exhaustive information about the bucket

post.description: Instruct Garage node(s) to retry the resynchronization of one or several missing data block(s).

parameters.description: Node ID to query, or `*` for all nodes, or `self` for the node responding to the request
parameters.name: node

post.description: Clear staged layout changes

200.description: All pending changes to the cluster layout have been erased

post.description: Set the value for a worker variable, on one or several cluster nodes.

parameters.description: Node ID to query, or `*` for all nodes, or `self` for the node responding to the request
parameters.name: node

post.description: Updates information about the specified admin API token.

parameters.description: Admin API token ID
parameters.name: id

post.description:
All fields (`websiteAccess` and `quotas`) are optional.
If they are present, the corresponding modifications are applied to the bucket, otherwise nothing is changed.

In `websiteAccess`: if `enabled` is `true`, `indexDocument` must be specified.
The field `errorDocument` is optional, if no error document is set a generic
error message is displayed when errors happen. Conversely, if `enabled` is
`false`, neither `indexDocument` nor `errorDocument` must be specified.

In `quotas`: new values of `maxSize` and `maxObjects` must both be specified, or set to `null`
to remove the quotas. An absent value will be considered the same as a `null`. It is not possible
to change only one of the two quotas.

parameters.description: ID of the bucket to update
parameters.name: id

post.description:
Send modifications to the cluster layout. These modifications will be included in the staged role changes, visible in subsequent calls of `GET /GetClusterHealth`. Once the set of staged changes is satisfactory, the user may call `POST /ApplyClusterLayout` to apply the changed changes, or `POST /RevertClusterLayout` to clear all of the staged changes in the layout.

Setting the capacity to `null` will configure the node as a gateway.
Otherwise, capacity must be now set in bytes (before Garage 0.9 it was arbitrary weights).
For example to declare 100GB, you must set `capacity: 100000000000`.

Garage uses internally the International System of Units (SI), it assumes that 1kB = 1000 bytes, and displays storage as kB, MB, GB (and not KiB, MiB, GiB that assume 1KiB = 1024 bytes).

requestBody.description:
To add a new node to the layout or to change the configuration of an existing node, simply set the values you want (`zone`, `capacity`, and `tags`).
To remove a node, simply pass the `remove: true` field.
This logic is represented in OpenAPI with a 'One Of' object.

Contrary to the CLI that may update only a subset of the fields capacity, zone and tags, when calling this API all of these values must be specified.

200.description: Proposed changes have been added to the list of pending changes

post.description:
Updates information about the specified API access key.

*Note: the secret key is not returned in the response, `null` is sent instead.*

parameters.description: Access key ID
parameters.name: id
